data.title: Small-cap communication services stocks with A grade EPS revisions as earnings season begins
data.description:
As Q1 2026 earnings season gets underway, the communication services sector enters reporting season navigating a complex mix of tailwinds and headwinds.

Within the small-cap segment of communication services, upward EPS revisions are a particularly meaningful signal that analyst sentiment is turning constructive ahead of Q1 reports. 

The following list highlights small-cap communication services stocks that have earned a Seeking Alpha EPS Revision Quant Grade of A- or higher, spanning integrated telecommunications, advertising technology, interactive media, and publishing, with market capitalizations ranging from approximately $300 million to $1.25 billion, and four companies, including ATN International and IDT Corporation, earning the highest possible A+ grade.

The list represents a variety of industries within the communication services sector, including integrated telecommunications, publishing, advertising, and interactive media. While the top four stocks hold A+ grades, the list remains competitive with several other companies like Angel Studios, Inc. (ANGX), Anterix Inc. (ATEX), and EverQuote, Inc. (EVER) maintaining solid A grades, and others such as Bumble Inc. (BMBL) and MediaAlpha, Inc. (MAX) earning A- grades.

  1. ATN International, Inc. (ATNI) – EPS revision grade: A+

  2. IDT Corporation (IDT) – EPS revision grade: A+

  3. National CineMedia, Inc. (NCMI) – EPS revision grade: A+

  4. Scholastic Corporation (SCHL) – EPS revision grade: A+

  5. Angel Studios, Inc. (ANGX) – EPS revision grade: A

  6. Anterix Inc. (ATEX) – EPS revision grade: A

  7. EverQuote, Inc. (EVER) – EPS revision grade: A

  8. Nextdoor Holdings, Inc. (NXDR) – EPS revision grade: A

  9. Bumble Inc. (BMBL) – EPS revision grade: A-

  10. MediaAlpha, Inc. (MAX) – EPS revision grade: A-

  11. PubMatic, Inc. (PUBM) – EPS revision grade: A-

  12. QuinStreet, Inc. (QNST) – EPS revision grade: A-

  13. Reservoir Media, Inc. (RSVR) – EPS revision grade: A-

  14. Sinclair, Inc. (SBGI) – EPS revision grade: A-

  15. Taboola.com Ltd. (TBLA) – EPS revision grade: A-

Communication Services ETFs: (XLC), (VOX), (IYZ), (RSPC), and (XTL)

data.title: Performance Shipping to sell Aframax Vessel M/T P. Aliki for $42.6M
data.description:
Performance Shipping (PSHG) has signed a memorandum of agreement to sell its oldest vessel, the 2010-built Aframax tanker vessel, M/T P. Aliki, to Trafigura Maritime Logistics for $42.65M.

The company acquired the M/T P. Aliki in the fourth quarter of 2022 for a gross purchase price of $36.5M. The vessel currently serves as part of the collateral for the company’s outstanding credit facility with Alpha Bank S.A.

Part of the net proceeds from the sale will be applied to repay ~$12.8M in accordance with the terms of the loan agreement.

The vessel is expected to be delivered to the new owners in or around the end of the third quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and upon expiration of her current US$30,000 per day time charter with Pakistan National Shipping.

data.title: IonQ surges after getting DARPA contract
data.description:
IonQ (IONQ) said it has been awarded a contract in the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency's (DARPA) Heterogeneous Architectures for Quantum (HARQ) program.

Shares of the quantum computing company surged about 10% on Tuesday.

The company noted that the HARQ contract will support IonQ's development of high-speed interconnects capable of linking multiple types of quantum computers.

As a part of HARQ, IonQ plays a key role in enabling a new class of networked quantum computers that combine distinct qubit types — such as trapped ions, neutral atoms, and/or superconducting qubits — into an interconnected, high-performance architecture, according to the company.

IonQ said that this effort seeks to use advances in photonic integration and quantum interconnects for reliable communication between diverse qubit species.

"We look forward to collaborating with DARPA to strengthen national security by developing the quantum platform which can serve as a backbone for networking and scaling quantum systems - across qubit types - for advanced public and private sector applications," said IonQ's Chairman and CEO Niccolo de Masi.

DARPA is an independent research and development agency within the U.S. Department of War.

data.title: Crowded trades shift to oil and semiconductors as gold loses top spot
data.description:
Investors are crowding into oil and semiconductor trades, according to Bank of America’s latest global fund manager survey, underscoring how positioning is converging around a narrow set of macro and technology themes.

A net 24% of respondents identified “long oil” as the most crowded trade, while an equal share pointed to “long global semiconductors,” leaving the two tied at the top in April. The shift highlights continued conviction in energy markets alongside persistent enthusiasm for chip-related exposure tied to structural demand trends.

The positioning marks a notable change from the previous month, when “long gold” and “long global semiconductors” dominated as the most crowded trades, each cited by 35% of investors. The drop in gold’s prominence suggests a rotation away from traditional defensive plays even as geopolitical risks remain elevated.

Beyond the top trades, the survey shows a steep fall-off in crowding elsewhere. “Long gold” now ranks third at 15%, followed by “long Magnificent 7” at 11%. On the bearish side, smaller shares of investors flagged “short global consumer discretionary” and “short private credit” as crowded positions, both at 9%.

The results point to increasingly concentrated positioning across asset classes, with investors leaning into a mix of commodity exposure and technology leadership themes rather than broad diversification.

Bank of America said the findings are based on its April survey of global fund managers.

Here is a chart from BofA:

data.title: Gas price shock will only partly offset tax refunds - JPMorgan
data.description:
Surging gasoline (UNG) costs induced by the war in Iran are eating into the financial lift that U.S. households are getting from bigger income tax refunds this year, though most taxpayers will still come out ahead, according to a new analysis from JPMorgan Asset Management.

The firm’s research shows that every income group retains some degree of residual stimulus after accounting for higher fuel expenses, but the cushion varies dramatically depending on where households fall on the income ladder—painting a lopsided picture for consumers. 

Middle-income households appear to fare the worst in relative terms. For those in the 40%–60% and 60%–80% income brackets, higher gasoline prices absorb nearly all the benefit from larger tax refunds. Lower-income households end up slightly better off, but only modestly so.

The biggest winners are at the top. JPMorgan’s estimates indicate that the top 20% of earners receive the largest refund increases by a wide margin. Even after factoring in elevated fuel costs, they retain the strongest positive financial impulse of any group.

data.title: Geopolitical conflicts overshadow inflation as the top market threat, according to BofA
data.description:
Global investors are increasingly positioning for geopolitical shocks, according to the latest Fund Manager Survey from Bank of America, which highlights a notable shift in perceived tail risks.

The survey shows that 44% of respondents now identify geopolitical conflict as the most significant “tail risk,” marking the second consecutive month it has held the top spot. This represents a sharp rise from February, underscoring how quickly global tensions have moved to the forefront of investor concerns as the Middle East conflict between the U.S. and Iran continues. The surge also suggests growing unease around international stability and its potential to disrupt markets.

Inflation remains the second-largest risk, cited by 26% of fund managers, reflecting persistent concerns that price pressures could remain elevated longer than expected. Meanwhile, fears around a disorderly rise in bond yields ranked third at 9%, pointing to continued sensitivity around interest rate volatility and fixed income markets. 

Other risks appear more contained. Private credit concerns were flagged by 8% of respondents, while only 5% pointed to a potential artificial intelligence bubble—down significantly from prior months. Political risks such as U.S. midterm elections and trade war tensions were considered minimal, each garnering just low single-digit responses. 

Overall, the data signals a market increasingly driven by macro and geopolitical uncertainty rather than purely economic factors. 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ). 

Fixed Income ETFs: (TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), (BIL), (AGG), (BND), (VCIT), (MUB), (MBB), (JNK), (LQD), (HYG), (VTIP), (TIP), (SCHP), (STIP), (TIPX), (SPIP), (WIP), (GTIP), (LQDI), and (RINF).

data.title: Fastly gets a rating cut at Craig-Hallum
data.description:
Craig-Hallum downgraded Fastly (FSLY) to Hold from Buy but increased the price target on the stock to $24 from $15, noting that it awaits better risk-reward as AI impact looms.

Shares of Fastly climbed about 2% premarket on Tuesday. 

Analysts led by Jeff Van Rhee said risk/reward now appears considerably less attractive as a range of risks are rising in relevance.

 "For the negatives/concerns: first, capex (and increasingly, margin) is going to be challenged by surging prices for memory and other key network hardware, as demand from Al infrastructure buildouts exceeds supply. Second, Al has now clearly surpassed human capabilities with respect to detection and exploitation of security vulnerabilities in virtually all systems, making it increasingly hard to charge for value-add services layered on top of the core network (i.e. WAF, etc), with that value more likely now to accrue to leading frontier models (security currently 22% of revenue)," said the analysts.

Thirdly, the analysts noted that compares will get more difficult, mainly as Edgio tailwinds abate. In December 2024, Akamai Technologies acquired select assets of Edgio, leading many customers to churn off, likely landing on Fastly's platform at renewal time, the analysts added.

In addition, the analysts said that the magnitude of improvement in AI represented by Anthropic's Mythos (over the already surprising November 25 release of Opus 4.5) not only casts doubt on the leading security solutions, but has also set a new slope to the curve of improvement in AI capabilities, meaningfully increasing the probability of disruption over the next few releases as they again make unexpected leaps.

The analysts also highlighted some positives. 

Firstly, Van Rhee and his team said that pricing remains compelling, with mid-single-digit declines yielding the best gross margin in years. Secondly, the analysts noted that remaining performance obligations, or RPOs, have surged, with at least a meaningful portion related to efforts to extract higher minimum-commitments from customers (increasing visibility). Thirdly, the analysts noted that Al is comprising an increased percentage of overall traffic, as bots/agents perform tasks

"In sum, the company has done a very nice job executing, integrating product and improving go-to-market. At the same time, we are stepping aside as much appears reflected in the current valuation and we await a more attractive risk/reward entry," said Van Rhee and his team.

data.title: Camtek to acquire Visual Layer to expand AI inspection capabilities
data.description:
• Camtek (CAMT) on Tuesday said it has signed a definitive agreement to acquire Visual Layer, a Tel Aviv-based artificial intelligence firm specializing in visual analytics.
  • Camtek said the acquisition will strengthen its inspection and metrology offerings for the semiconductor industry by integrating Visual Layer’s AI and visual intelligence technologies.
  • The company said it has already collaborated with Visual Layer for over a year and expects the deal to close in the coming weeks, subject to customary conditions.
  • Financial terms were not disclosed.
  • CAMT +5.52% premarket to $184.6.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CareCloud secures $50M credit facility, redeems preferred stock
data.description:
• CareCloud (CCLDO) on Tuesday said it has secured a $50M credit facility with Citizens Bank and Provident Bank, while also announcing plans to fully redeem its Series B preferred stock.
  • The company said the credit facility, led by Citizens Bank, will support its operations and growth initiatives.
  • CareCloud also said it will redeem all outstanding Series B preferred shares on May 15, eliminating about $3.2 million in annual dividend obligations and simplifying its capital structure.
  • The company said the moves are aimed at lowering financing costs, improving liquidity, and supporting expansion of its AI-driven healthcare technology platform.
  • CCLD -1.42% premarket to $2.76.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BlackRock's iShares just had its best Q1 ever: Here are the 10 top-rated funds in the lineup
data.description:
BlackRock's iShares unit just posted a record-breaking Q1, pulling in $132 billion in net inflows, the highest Q1 figure in iShares history, as part of a strong overall earnings beat for parent company BlackRock (BLK), which reported 8% year-over-year organic fee growth and adjusted margins expanding by more than 100 basis points. 

In light of this, below is a list of the top 10 iShares ETFs ranked according to their Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings. These traditional BlackRock fund products span various sectors including Information Technology, Natural Resources, Gold & Precious Metals, and regional equity markets such as South Korea, Brazil, and Austria.

The list is led by the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), which earned a near-perfect Quant Rating of 4.88. The iShares MSCI South Korea ETF (EWY) follows closely behind with a score of 4.85, while the iShares MSCI Peru and Global Exposure ETF (EPU) rounds out the top three with a rating of 4.69.

Metals and mining ETFs are well represented on the list, with the iShares MSCI Global Silver and Metals Miners ETF (SLVP) and the iShares Copper and Metals Mining ETF (ICOP) both earning Strong Buy ratings. Regional ETFs focused on Poland (EPOL), Brazil (EWZ), and Austria (EWO) also made the top ten, each earning solid Buy ratings above 4.4.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system grades ETFs based on their relative performance on various critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, momentum, and profitability. Ratings are given on a scale of 1 to 5, with any score of 3.5 or above considered bullish and any rating of 2.5 or below considered bearish.

Here is the list:

  1. iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), Quant Rating: 4.88

  2. iShares MSCI South Korea ETF (EWY), Quant Rating: 4.85

  3. iShares MSCI Peru and Global Exposure ETF (EPU), Quant Rating: 4.69

  4. iShares MSCI Global Silver and Metals Miners ETF (SLVP), Quant Rating: 4.61

  5. iShares US Oil Equipment & Services ETF (IEZ), Quant Rating: 4.57

  6. iShares MSCI Global Gold Miners ETF (RING), Quant Rating: 4.52

  7. iShares Copper and Metals Mining ETF (ICOP), Quant Rating: 4.51

  8. iShares MSCI Poland ETF (EPOL), Quant Rating: 4.46

  9. iShares MSCI Brazil ETF (EWZ), Quant Rating: 4.45

  10. iShares MSCI Austria ETF (EWO), Quant Rating: 4.44
